You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: 76016af2).

## Issue to Resolve

")" unexpectedly indented in expression but not in `if`
Given ktlint 0.39.0 and some code that looks like this:

```
val pass = if (condition1) {                 
        (
            condition2
        )
    } else {
        condition3
    }
```

## Expected Behavior:
this code seems to be formatted reasonably to us; we think ktlint should report no problems.

## Observed Behavior:
Instead we're observing it to request indenting "condition2" and ")" to get this:

```
val pass = if (condition1) {                 
        (
                condition2
            )
    } else {
        condition3
    }
```

At first I thought this seemed reasonable to me because it was presumably being treated as a line continuation, but then I checked what would happen if condition1 were multiple lines, and found in that case that ktlint requested that the parentheses of the `if` statement should be lined up:

```
if (
    condition1
) { // indendation matching the corresponding "("
    (
            condition2
        ) // indentation not matching the corresponding "("
} else {
    condition3
}
```

## Your Environment
We encounter this when upgrading from ktlint 0.36.0 to 0.39.0

The full code in question: https://android-review.googlesource.com/c/platform/frameworks/support/+/1432017/2/compose/animation/animation-core/src/test/java/androidx/compose/animation/core/SpringEstimationTest.kt#139

## Expected Behavior:

## Observed Behavior:

```

    } else {
        condition3
    }
```

At first I thought this seemed reasonable to me because it was presumably being treated as a line continuation, but then I checked what would happen if condition1 were multiple lines, and found in that case that ktlint requested that the parentheses of the `if` statement should be lined up:

```
if (
    condition1
) { // indendation matching the corresponding "("
    (
            condition2
        ) // indentation not matching the corresponding "("
} else {
    condition3
}
```

## Your Environment
We encounter this when upgrading from ktlint 0.36.0 to 0.39.0

The full code in question: https://android-review.googlesource.com/c/platform/frameworks/support/+/1432017/2/compose/animation/animation-core/src/test/java/androidx/compose/animation/core/SpringEstimationTest.kt#139

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

That is a known quirk of the Kotlin ide plugin formatter (whose behavior we are trying to match), please check #810 for details. I've also filed an issue on [youtrack](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-41316) and have to close this until further notice from JB, as it seems to be expected behavior.
I gave that YouTrack issue a thumbs up, which brought the total number of thumbs up to two. It's been two years, perhaps it's worth considering whether it could be okay to allow deviating from the IDE formatter in cases such as this? This looks super weird đź¤Ş
I also gave it a thumbs up. Any chance we could at least add a flag to allow deviation?
Reopened issue for investigation. If it gets solved, it will only be solved for the new code style `ktlint_official`.

 186â†’                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
 187â†’                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)

 189â†’                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)

 194â†’
 195â†’        when {
 196â†’            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
 197â†’                line++
 198â†’                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
 199â†’                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()

 203â†’                }

 205â†’            }
 206â†’
 207â†’            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||

 217â†’            node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY -> {

 220â†’                    // is followed by another super type:
 221â†’                    //      class Foo(

 245â†’            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->

 253â†’                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 254â†’                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
 255â†’                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
 256â†’                node.elementType == USER_TYPE ->

 259â†’            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 260â†’                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
 261â†’                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->

 266â†’
 267â†’            node.elementType == IF ->

 269â†’
 270â†’            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 271â†’                visitLbrace(node)
 272â†’
 273â†’            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 274â†’                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 275â†’                startIndentContext(
 276â†’                    fromAstNode = node,
 277â†’                    lastChildIndent = "",
 278â†’                )
 279â†’
 280â†’            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 281â†’                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 282â†’                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 283â†’
 284â†’            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 285â†’                visitValueParameter(node)
 286â†’
 287â†’            node.elementType == FUN ->

 289â†’
 290â†’            node.elementType == CLASS ->

 293â†’            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->

 295â†’
 296â†’            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 297â†’                visitBinaryExpression(node)

 299â†’            node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION -> {
 300â†’                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 301â†’                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 302â†’                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&

 306â†’                    // val fooBar2 = foo

 310â†’                    startIndentContext(

 318â†’                } else {

 322â†’
 323â†’            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 324â†’                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->

 327â†’            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&

 330â†’
 331â†’            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 332â†’                visitWhen(node)
 333â†’
 334â†’            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 335â†’                visitWhenEntry(node)
 336â†’
 337â†’            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 338â†’                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 339â†’                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 340â†’
 341â†’            node.elementType == KDOC ->

 343â†’
 344â†’            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||
 345â†’                node.elementType == TYPEALIAS ->
 346â†’                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 347â†’
 348â†’            node.elementType == FOR ||
 349â†’                node.elementType == WHILE ->

 352â†’            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 353â†’                visitLBracket(node)
 354â†’
 355â†’            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->

 358â†’            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION ->
 359â†’                visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)

 361â†’            node.elementType == TRY ->

 364â†’            else -> {

 453â†’    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 454â†’        // Outer indent context

 459â†’        startIndentContext(

 462â†’            firstChildIndent = "",
 463â†’            lastChildIndent = "",
 464â†’        )

 466â†’        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 467â†’        node
 468â†’            .treeParent
 469â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 470â†’            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 471â†’            ?.let { arrow ->

 485â†’    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 486â†’        if (isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 487â†’            // val fieldExample =
 488â†’            //      LongNameClass {
 489â†’            //              paramA,
 490â†’            //              paramB,
 491â†’            //              paramC ->
 492â†’            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 493â†’            //      }
 494â†’            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 495â†’        } else {
 496â†’            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 497â†’            // val fieldExample =
 498â†’            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 499â†’            //                     paramB,
 500â†’            //                     paramC ->
 501â†’            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 502â†’            //     }
 503â†’            // val fieldExample =
 504â†’            //     someFunction(
 505â†’            //         1,
 506â†’            //         2,
 507â†’            //     ) { paramA,
 508â†’            //         paramB,
 509â†’            //         paramC ->
 510â†’            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 511â†’            //     }
 512â†’            this
 513â†’                .takeIf { it.isPartOf(CALL_EXPRESSION) }
 514â†’                ?.treeParent
 515â†’                ?.leaves(false)
 516â†’                ?.takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
 517â†’                ?.sumOf { it.textLength }
 518â†’                ?.plus(2) // need to add spaces to compensate for "{ "
 519â†’                ?.let { length -> " ".repeat(length) }
 520â†’                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 521â†’        }
